intent: Define the SecurityRecipes customer proof pack so the secure context layer can move from credible reference evidence to acquisition-grade customer evidence. The pack binds design-partner telemetry, MCP authorization decisions, run receipts, redaction, ROI metrics, renewal gates, and buyer proof claims into one generated artifact without pretending customer runtime data already exists.

positioning.buyer: Frontier model labs, AI platform vendors, security platform vendors, regulated enterprises, VC diligence teams, and strategic acquirers
positioning.category: Customer proof contract for secure context, MCP governance, and agentic AI assurance
positioning.enterprise_promise: SecurityRecipes makes AI easier to approve by telling a buyer exactly which runtime events, metrics, receipts, and redacted evidence must be collected before a design-partner pilot can support renewal or acquisition claims.
positioning.why_now: Agentic systems are moving from pilots to production MCP, tool use, A2A handoffs, non-human identities, browser agents, and private context. The market now needs customer-proof evidence, not only standards mapping or prompt libraries.

source_references.name: OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications
source_references.why_it_matters: OWASP frames agent behavior hijacking, tool misuse, and identity abuse as real-world agentic risks that customer proof must measure.

source_references.name: OWASP MCP Top 10
source_references.why_it_matters: MCP-specific risks such as token exposure, scope creep, command execution, missing telemetry, shadow servers, and context over-sharing drive the proof events in this pack.

source_references.name: Model Context Protocol Authorization
source_references.why_it_matters: Protected MCP servers need resource indicators, token audience validation, PKCE, secure token handling, and scope minimization before customer proof can count a tool call as governed.

source_references.name: OpenTelemetry Semantic Conventions for MCP
source_references.why_it_matters: MCP spans and metrics are the emerging evidence language for customer proof because they capture sessions, methods, transports, tool calls, errors, and durations.

source_references.name: NIST AI RMF Generative AI Profile
source_references.why_it_matters: The GenAI profile supports governance, measurement, monitoring, provenance, incident response, and lifecycle risk management expectations that design partners will ask to see.

source_references.name: NIST AI RMF Critical Infrastructure Profile concept note
source_references.why_it_matters: NIST's 2026 critical-infrastructure AI work raises the bar for customer proof in high-assurance environments where agentic AI can touch essential operations.

source_references.name: AI Data Security: Best Practices for Securing Data Used to Train and Operate AI Systems
source_references.publisher: CISA, NSA, FBI, and international partners
source_references.why_it_matters: Customer proof must show data security, integrity, monitoring, threat detection, and lifecycle controls for the data that powers AI systems.

source_references.name: Joint Guidance on Deploying AI Systems Securely
source_references.publisher: CISA, NSA, FBI, ASD, CCCS, NCSC-NZ, and NCSC-UK
source_references.why_it_matters: Deployment guidance emphasizes confidentiality, integrity, availability, vulnerability mitigation, detection, and response for AI systems and related data.

proof_success_criteria: Every counted customer run has a receipt, context hash, policy decision, authorization decision, verifier result, and reviewer outcome.
ROI metrics are calculated from customer runtime events instead of assumed productivity.
Redaction and data-boundary rules are explicit enough for regulated telemetry pipelines.
Safe holds, denials, and kill decisions are counted as product value when they prevent unsafe agent action.
Renewal evidence identifies budget owner, paid wedge, expansion trigger, and support burden.
Acquisition claims remain blocked until customer evidence is present, redacted, reproducible, and linked to generated reference packs.

evidence_sources.title: Enterprise Trust Center Export
evidence_sources.proof_role: Reference evidence index for trust-center sections, diligence questions, runtime evidence contract, and pack hashes.

evidence_sources.title: Secure Context Value Model
evidence_sources.proof_role: Assumption-based ROI model that customer telemetry must replace before renewal or acquisition claims.

evidence_sources.title: Design Partner Pilot Pack
evidence_sources.proof_role: Pilot phases, required telemetry, success metrics, monetization wedges, and risk gates.

evidence_sources.title: Agentic Telemetry Contract
evidence_sources.proof_role: OpenTelemetry-aligned signal and redaction contract for runtime proof.

evidence_sources.title: Agentic Run Receipt Pack
evidence_sources.proof_role: Receipt structure for reconstructing identity, context, MCP decisions, approvals, verifiers, evidence, and closure.

evidence_sources.title: MCP Authorization Conformance Pack
evidence_sources.proof_role: Runtime evidence for resource indicators, token audience, token passthrough, session binding, and scope drift.

evidence_sources.title: Agentic Protocol Conformance Pack
evidence_sources.proof_role: MCP and A2A protocol controls, source references, and conformance checks.

evidence_sources.title: Agentic App Intake Pack
evidence_sources.proof_role: Launch review decisions for autonomy, data, tools, memory, guardrails, telemetry, and approval evidence.

evidence_sources.title: Agentic Source Freshness Watch
evidence_sources.proof_role: Current-source and standards-drift evidence for buyer-facing claims.

evidence_sources.title: Secure Context Eval Pack
evidence_sources.proof_role: Eval scenarios for retrieval correctness, attestation, poisoning resilience, egress, answers, and handoff boundaries.

proof_claims.title: Governed context retrieval is working in customer runs
proof_claims.buyer_question: Can the buyer prove what context an agent saw, why it was allowed, and whether the source was current?
proof_claims.blocked_until: At least ten customer runs return registered context with hashes, freshness state, citations, and receipt linkage.

proof_claims.title: MCP calls are authorized before execution
proof_claims.buyer_question: Can protected MCP calls enforce resource, audience, scope, session, and token-handling controls?
proof_claims.blocked_until: Customer MCP calls produce pre-execution authorization decisions and no token passthrough or unbound audience is counted as success.

proof_claims.title: Unsafe agent actions hold or stop cleanly
proof_claims.buyer_question: Does the system create measurable value by stopping risky agent behavior instead of only celebrating successful automation?
proof_claims.blocked_until: Pilot data includes safe holds, denials, or kill decisions tied to receipts and reviewer outcomes.

proof_claims.title: Telemetry is useful without leaking sensitive data
proof_claims.buyer_question: Can security teams reconstruct agent runs without turning observability into a secret, prompt, or private-code leakage sink?
proof_claims.blocked_until: Customer telemetry shows metadata-first events, redaction state, receipt linkage, and zero counted secret captures.

proof_claims.title: ROI assumptions are replaced by customer telemetry
proof_claims.buyer_question: What proves value beyond assumed productivity or demo metrics?
proof_claims.blocked_until: Customer run data replaces default value-model assumptions for at least one renewal scenario.

proof_claims.title: Source and standards freshness is maintained
proof_claims.buyer_question: Can the buyer see whether SecurityRecipes guidance stays current as MCP, OWASP, NIST, and AI data-security guidance changes?
proof_claims.blocked_until: Freshness watch shows no due critical sources and every customer-facing pack has a regeneration path.

proof_claims.title: Renewal and paid wedge are explicit
proof_claims.buyer_question: Which paid product surface did the customer validate, and who owns the budget?
proof_claims.blocked_until: The design partner names a paid wedge, budget owner, expansion trigger, and support burden before any acquisition-ready claim is made.

metric_definitions.title: Safe hold and deny count
metric_definitions.target: All ambiguous or unsafe states hold, deny, or kill instead of silently proceeding

metric_definitions.title: Automation success ratio
metric_definitions.target: >= 0.50 of eligible pilot findings produce approved output or structured triage

metric_definitions.title: Source refresh due count
metric_definitions.target: 0 critical due sources before buyer export

metric_definitions.title: Paid wedge confirmation
metric_definitions.target: Named wedge, owner, expansion trigger, and renewal decision

renewal_gates.title: Evidence complete
renewal_gates.what_passes: Core run receipts, context hashes, and MCP decisions meet target coverage.

renewal_gates.title: Security value proven
renewal_gates.what_passes: The pilot shows risky actions stop safely and sensitive telemetry escapes stay at zero.

renewal_gates.title: ROI replaces assumptions
renewal_gates.what_passes: Customer telemetry can replace at least one value-model scenario.

renewal_gates.title: Freshness ready
renewal_gates.what_passes: Critical source-backed packs are current or explicitly reviewed before buyer export.

renewal_gates.title: Paid wedge ready
renewal_gates.what_passes: Design partner names the paid wedge, budget owner, expansion trigger, and support burden.

acquirer_readout.headline: SecurityRecipes has reference evidence; the customer proof pack defines exactly what must be measured before a $10-20M outcome is credible.
acquirer_readout.what_is_ready: Open corpus, generated evidence packs, deterministic evaluators, read-only MCP access, source freshness, buyer brief, value model, and pilot motion.
acquirer_readout.what_is_not_ready: Hosted tenant isolation, customer-private evidence ingestion, live connector monitoring, signed receipts, paid renewal signal, metering, and customer references.
next_90_days: Run one design partner around read-only secure context and MCP authorization proof.
Bind customer-private context with hashes, owners, freshness, poisoning scan summary, and redacted telemetry.
Collect enough receipt, MCP decision, verifier, reviewer, and ROI events to replace one value-model scenario.
Use renewal gates to block overclaiming until the paid wedge and budget owner are explicit.

risk_register.risk: Demo or synthetic run metrics are mistaken for customer proof.
risk_register.mitigation: Label all value-model assumptions as assumptions and require tenant-bound runtime events before renewal or acquisition claims.

risk_register.risk: Proof telemetry captures secrets, private code, raw prompts, or customer logs.
risk_register.mitigation: Use metadata, hashes, decisions, redacted summaries, and receipt ids by default; never count runs with raw secret capture.

risk_register.risk: MCP calls are counted as governed without pre-execution authorization decisions.
risk_register.mitigation: Require resource indicator, token audience, scope state, session binding, and authorization decision fields for counted tool calls.

risk_register.risk: The project claims productivity gains without reviewer, verifier, and customer outcome data.
risk_register.mitigation: Require reviewer minutes, avoided engineer hours, verifier status, accepted output, and customer outcome fields.

risk_register.risk: Fast-moving MCP, OWASP, NIST, CISA, or telemetry guidance makes customer-facing proof stale.
risk_register.mitigation: Bind customer proof exports to source freshness checks and regenerate evidence before trust-center delivery.

risk_register.risk: The pilot produces useful activity but no obvious paid product surface.
risk_register.mitigation: Require paid wedge id, budget owner, renewal signal, expansion trigger, and support burden before expansion.
